stories.headline: Watch: Key moments from King Charles III’s address to US Congress
stories.summary: King Charles III addressed US Congress, stressing the importance of the UK-US partnership in facing global challenges. Lawmakers applauded as he mixed humor, history and forward-looking remarks in a rare speech by a British monarch.

stories.headline: Trump welcomes King Charles III and Queen Camilla for state dinner
stories.summary: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed Britain’s King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the White House for a state dinner in the East Room.

stories.headline: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
stories.summary: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

stories.headline: UN aid chief says situation in Somalia worsened by US war on Iran
stories.summary: UN relief coordinator Tom Fletcher has slammed the war on Iran during a visit to Somalia.

stories.headline: Komatsu expects profits to tumble 16% on Iran war, US tariffs
Australia’s Albanese Rules Out New Taxes on Gas Exports, Citing Security
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said his government wouldn’t “undermine” existing liquefied natural gas exports with new taxes, warning that doing so during a global energy crunch would risk investment and fuel security.

stories.headline: DOJ Secures New James Comey Indictment Over Alleged Threat Against Trump
stories.summary: The new case stems from a social media post showing seashells on a North Carolina beach that the Trump administration characterized as a threat against the president.

stories.headline: King Charles urges checks on executive power as Trump hosts royal visit
stories.summary: In a speech to Congress, the monarch avoided naming the president but highlighted democratic norms, Ukraine and Western unity.

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese rules out gas export tax on existing contracts and criticises ‘populist’ campaign
stories.summary: Prime minister says the middle of a global fuel crisis is ‘the worst possible time to jeopardise’ Australia’s partnerships with Asian trading partnersFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe prime minister, Anthony Albanese, has confirmed next month’s federal budget will not include a new tax on existing gas export contracts as he criticised the “populist” campaign callin

stories.headline: Trump to feature on limited-edition US passports for 250th anniversary
stories.summary: The passports are part of broader plans to celebrate the milestone anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.headline: UAE quits OPEC as oil cartel takes blow during war on Iran
stories.summary: The UAE’s decision to quit OPEC to prioritise its ‘national interests’ deals a blow to the oil group.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says Tehran wants end to blockade; Israel kills medics
stories.summary: US president says Iran has reached out and asked Washington to lift its naval blockade on Iranian ports.

stories.headline: Europe revives renewables and nuclear to address the energy crisis
stories.summary: Brussels is looking for a long-term fix, putting both renewables and nuclear back on the table after the Strait of Hormuz closure sent energy bills soaring. Watch the video!

stories.headline: Qatar rolls out business relief measures amid Iran War and regional crisis
stories.summary: Qatar has introduced new measures to maintain market stability and reinforce investor confidence as regional tensions weigh on economic sentiment.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight in southern Lebanon, including three rescue workers
stories.summary: Israel has been fighting Hezbollah since early March, sending troops into south Lebanon to battle the Iran-backed militant group, with the violence ongoing despite a shaky ongoing ceasefire.

stories.headline: UBS trading gains fuel 80% profit surge
stories.summary: Market volatility from Middle East war propels strong activity at Swiss bank

stories.headline: Can Opec survive the UAE’s exit?
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi’s surprise decision to leave the oil cartel raises doubts over its future

stories.headline: Handjani: OPEC Will Move Forward Weaker
stories.summary: The UAE has made a shock exit from OPEC blindsiding its partners and diluting the cartel's ability to steer oil prices via supply cuts. Amir Handjani, Non-resident fellow at the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft spoke to Bloomberg's Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the move and whether it was a political or economical decision. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB Finds No Clear Case to Hike Yet on Lane’s Data Dashboard
stories.summary: The array of data the European Central Bank is scrutinizing to pinpoint economic damage from the Iran war is signaling that there’s no cast-iron case to lift interest rates just yet.

stories.headline: Chip Supply Chains Risk Being Next Casualty of War
stories.summary: The question is whether companies have enough stockpiles on hand to ride out the blockade.

stories.headline: Full Guest List for Trump’s State Dinner With Charles and Camilla
stories.summary: Six members of the Supreme Court were invited to the dinner, alongside top administration officials, billionaires and Republican lawmakers.

stories.headline: Beneath King Charles’s Jokes and Decorum, Some Subtle Rebuttals to Trump
stories.summary: King Charles III gently pushed back against President Trump’s attacks on Britain and NATO, and he spoke of the importance of checks and balances.

stories.headline: Organisers of high-level climate summit in Colombia say 'we must transition away from fossil fuels'
stories.summary: The two-day segment of ministers and senior officials marks the political centerpiece of the First Conference on Transitioning away from Fossil Fuels, where more than 50 countries have been discussing how to move away from oil, gas and coal.

stories.headline: The great commodities disruption
stories.summary: Trump’s attack on Iran has served as a reminder of how interconnected the global economy remains

stories.headline: Central Banks ‘Scoop Up a Load’ of Gold in Bumpy First Quarter
stories.summary: Central banks added gold holdings at the fastest pace in more than a year in the first quarter, as a slump in prices encouraged a wave of buying that more than offset sales by a handful of institutions.

stories.headline: Deutsche CFO: Trading Started ‘Constructively’ in April
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG Chief Financial Officer Raja Akram discusses the lender’s performance in the first quarter as it posted higher-than-expected profit and revenue as income from trading held up and it earned more from asset management and retail banking. Akram says April “started constructively” after some jitters in March. He also discusses private credit and the uncertainty around the situation in the Middle East. Akram speaks to Bloomberg’s Oliv

stories.headline: Finland’s Kone Agrees to Buy Rival TK Elevator for €29.4 Billion
stories.summary: Kone Oyj agreed to acquire TK Elevator for €29.4 billion ($34.4 billion) including debt, in what will be one of Europe’s biggest-ever private equity exits.

stories.headline: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall as Lloyds and Astra Report Earnings
Deutsche Bank CFO Says Trading Business Started Well in April
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG saw its fixed income and currencies trading business start “constructively” in the second quarter after some jitters in March, Chief Financial Officer Raja Akram said in an interview.

stories.headline: S.Korea SME & Startup Ministry on Domestic Growth
stories.summary: Roh Yong-seok, Vice Minister of South Korea's SME and Startup Ministry, discusses the country's plans and strategy to support and grow domestic startups and enterprises, from injecting more funding for support and working with regional economies to bolster cooperation.  He speaks with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UAE Blindsides OPEC, OpenAI Sparks Tech Jitters | The Asia Trade 4/29/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China Rare Earth Shares Rally as Beijing Targets Output Breaches
stories.summary: Shares in China’s major rare earths producers advanced after the government unveiled detailed penalties for unauthorized production, marking a fresh effort to bolster Beijing’s “total control” regime for the geopolitically pivotal industry.

stories.headline: RemeGen Focused on Growing Domestic Business
stories.summary: Shaojing Tong, CFO at Chinese biotech firm RemeGen, discusses the company's first quarter results and shares his confidence in growth in the domestic market. The company's stock has outperformed all of its peers in the MSCI China Healthcare Index since the start of the year.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Rightwing provocateur’s plan to register ‘Free Palestine party’ renews concern over Victoria’s voting system
stories.summary: Labor under pressure to scrap group voting tickets as Avi Yemini details plan to funnel votes to conservative parties at state election A rightwing provocateur’s plan to register the “Free Palestine party” and use it to funnel votes to conservative parties in Victoria’s state election has renewed concern over the state’s voting system.Avi Yemini, a former Israeli solider and self-styled investigative journalist who creates content for his YouTube

stories.headline: Afghan students recount harrowing university strike
stories.summary: Afghan students have described the moment a missile hit their university. Officials blame Pakistan for Monday’s attack.

stories.headline: China accuses foreign forces of brainwashing youth into 'lying flat'
German-Indian submarine deal nears signing, as both eye strategic tie
Homegrown AI powers China's ambitions, from classroom to home
Foreign coffee chains take fresh crack at China via new cities, formats
Hyundai Motor leaves peers in the dust as AI bets fuel stock price surge
Mitsubishi Materials to lift tungsten carbide prices on tight China supply
Honda plays catch-up in Vietnam and India's e-motorcycle markets
What China holds in store for Apple's new CEO
BYD Q1 profit down 55% from year ago as it banks on overseas sales
King Charles defends transatlantic relationship in speech to Congress
stories.summary: UK monarch’s visit to Washington comes at a fraught moment between the nations amid US-Israeli war against Iran

stories.headline: Path cleared for Everest climbers after huge ice block
stories.summary: Experts warn there are still risks of further ice collapses, and there are fears climbers will again have to queue to reach the summit.

stories.headline: King Charles III Cracks Jokes and Wins Over Congress
stories.summary: Charles III’s address at a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday sprinkled well-crafted jokes among carefully chosen references to the United States and Britain’s shared history.

stories.headline: Congress cheers King Charles for underlining ‘checks and balances’
stories.summary: US lawmakers gave King Charles III a standing ovation upon mentioning the importance of “checks and balances”.

stories.headline: King Charles calls for NATO unity, Ukraine support in US Congress speech
stories.summary: UK sovereign hails national friendship, but delivers pointed messages in speech during four-day US visit.

stories.headline: Ex-FBI Director Comey indicted in probe over online post officials say constituted Trump threat
stories.summary: Trump fired Comey in May 2017 amid an FBI investigation into potential ties between Russia and Trump’s presidential campaign.

stories.headline: Mountaineer climbs Everest for Palestinian children
stories.summary: Mostafa Salameh, a mountaineer, is on a mission to carry letters from children in Gaza to the top of Mount Everest.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: Ukraine says it will open arms exports with 'Drone Deals,' but not to all countries
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise.

stories.headline: James Comey indicted over 2025 social media post allegedly threatening Trump
stories.summary: The former FBI director shared a photo showing seashells arranged to write out “86 47,” which some saw as a call for violence against President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: European parliament urges EU to draw up standardised consent-based definition of rape
stories.summary: Eight EU members continue to include force or violence in their definitions in national criminal codesThe European parliament has called on the EU to draw up a standardised consent-based definition of rape, in what legislators described as a crucial step towards addressing the patchwork of laws, some of them insufficient, that now exist across the bloc.On Tuesday, 447 of the parliament’s 720 MEPs voted to approve a report calling for a common def

stories.headline: Lebanon families face second displacement amid ongoing warnings
stories.summary: Families displaced from southern Lebanon set up tents again in Beirut after renewed Israeli warnings.

stories.headline: Watch: Elderly woman rescued by robot from Ukraine's frontline
stories.summary: The Ukrainian army spotted the woman using a drone and sent the machine to her rescue as she was trying to leave her war-torn village.

stories.headline: Swarm of 10,000 bees settles on bike outside Louvre in Paris
stories.summary: The swarm gathers underneath the saddle of a bike locked to the railings of a metro station.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
stories.summary: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.

stories.headline: Goldman stops bankers using Anthropic’s Claude in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Employees were unable to access company’s AI models as of a few weeks ago

stories.headline: Trump’s Face Will Appear on Limited-Edition U.S. Passports, State Dept. Says
stories.summary: The department said it planned to release “a limited number of specially designed” U.S. passports that feature a picture of the president to commemorate the country’s 250th anniversary in July.

stories.headline: Treasury Issues More Sanctions on Iranian Oil Exports
stories.summary: The measures aim to crack down on Iran’s shadow banking system and Chinese purchases of Iranian oil.

stories.headline: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
stories.summary: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle EastThree vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry.The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker c

stories.headline: European Parliament demands €200 billion extra for EU budget in hardball talks
stories.summary: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify for 2028-2034 cycle.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
stories.summary: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.headline: Nasdaq Futures Rise in Run-Up to Tech Results, Fed: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US equity-index futures rose as investors positioned ahead of a raft of earnings from the largest technology companies in what has been a generally upbeat reporting season. Markets are also awaiting a Federal Reserve policy decision.

stories.headline: Musk testifies at OpenAI trial it’s not OK to ‘loot a charity’
stories.summary: In his lawsuit, Musk said OpenAI cofounder and president betrayed him and public by turning into profit-seeking firm.

stories.headline: Thousands filled Amsterdam’s streets and canals to celebrate the king’s birthday
stories.summary: Orange crowds fill the streets of Amsterdam as part of the Dutch monarch Willem-Alexander's birthday celebrations.

stories.headline: German bid to rescue 'Timmy' the whale stranded off Baltic coast passes key hurdle
stories.summary: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.

stories.headline: King Charles’s rare state visit offers U.K. a chance to mend ties with Trump
stories.summary: The pageantry began Monday amid heightened security concerns and a growing rift over the Iran war. The U.K. hopes the president’s love of pomp and the king’s “poker face” can help heal their alliance.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
stories.summary: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flamesThe US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-P

stories.headline: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report
stories.summary: Regime used its isolation after closing borders to escalate killings when global scrutiny disappeared, NGO claimsNorth Korea dramatically increased its use of the death penalty after closing its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, using its isolation to escalate killings when international scrutiny disappeared, according to a report mapping 13 years of executions under the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un.The number of documented cases of executio

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.summary: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.summary: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.

stories.headline: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.headline: America’s descent into state capitalism is exaggerated
stories.summary: Particularly by the state
